name: Fort Rucker
tagline: The 'Home of Army Aviation,' where Army pilots earn their wings, in southeast Alabama's Wiregrass.
_note: Army aviation training post (southeast AL Wiregrass; Central time zone). Name history: Fort Rucker -> Fort Novosel (2023) -> Fort Rucker (June 2025, now honoring WWI aviator Capt. Edward W. Rucker, not the Confederate general). Facts verified from official sources (home.army.mil/rucker, Lyster AHC, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). BAH MHA AL002 (Fort Rucker) via the bah_rates single source of truth. Housing partner not named pending confirmation. Alabama is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 36330 (Enterprise).

data.Mission: U.S. Army Aviation Center of Excellence and flight training
data.Also hosts: Army Aeromedical Research Lab, School of Aviation Medicine, Aviation Museum
data.Note: Reverted from 'Fort Novosel' to 'Fort Rucker' in 2025 (new namesake)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Rucker is the home of Army Aviation, where the U.S. Army Aviation Center of Excellence trains the Army's helicopter pilots and develops aviation doctrine and tactics. Much of the population is here for flight school or as instructors.

It sits in the Wiregrass region of southeast Alabama, bordered by Daleville, Ozark, and Enterprise. The post carried the name Fort Novosel from 2023 to 2025 before reverting to Fort Rucker, now honoring a World War I aviator.

data.subtitle: Fort Rucker is an affordable, small-town aviation post. The main choice is which of the surrounding towns fits your commute and schools, with on-post housing also available.
intro: As the Army's flight-training hub, the community is full of student pilots and instructors, and the cost of living in the Wiregrass is low, so BAH stretches well.
Three towns border the post, each with its own feel and schools: Daleville is closest, Enterprise is the larger, family-popular choice, and Ozark sits on the north side.

recommendations.advice: Daleville borders the post for the shortest commute and the lowest costs.
Enterprise is the larger town, popular with families for its schools and shopping, a short drive away.
Ozark sits on the north side of the post and is another close, affordable community.
On-post housing is available; with low local costs, weigh it against renting or buying nearby.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Fort Rucker site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Fort Rucker has privatized on-post family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA AL002 (Fort Rucker)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Daleville City, Enterprise City, Ozark City, and Dale County schools serve the towns around the post, depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and specialty care, including aviation medicine; inpatient care via Wiregrass-area network hospitals

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the answer is mostly about how long you'll be here and how steady the local market is.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or you're here for flight school.
You want time to learn the area before committing to a town.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing in a small market.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable Wiregrass market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a town and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. The Wiregrass is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well, but resale can be slow in a small, training-driven market. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on Daleville, Enterprise, and Ozark before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Daleville, Enterprise, Ozark, or Dale County) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Lyster Army Health Clinic or network providers.

data.q: Is it Fort Rucker or Fort Novosel?
data.a: Fort Rucker, as of June 2025. It was Fort Novosel from 2023 to 2025; the name now honors a World War I aviator, Capt. Edward W. Rucker, rather than the Confederate general.
It's the Home of Army Aviation, the U.S. Army Aviation Center of Excellence, where Army helicopter pilots train. Many people are here for flight school or as instructors.
Daleville is closest; Enterprise is the larger, family-popular town for schools; Ozark sits on the north side. On-post housing is also available.
Fort Rucker is MHA AL002. Use the calculator above for your grade.
